steps.title: Read the y-intercept and slope sign
steps.content: The graph crosses the y-axis at $(0, -5)$, so the constant term is $-5$ (consistent with every choice). Because the line falls from left to right, the slope is negative, eliminating choices A and B.

steps.title: Use a second labeled point
steps.content:
The line also passes through $(7, -8)$. Test the two negative-slope candidates:
Choice C: $f(7) = -\frac{3}{7}(7) - 5 = -3 - 5 = -8.$ ✓
Choice D: $f(7) = -\frac{7}{3}(7) - 5 = -\frac{49}{3} - 5 \approx -21.3.$ ✗

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• A ($\frac{3}{7}x - 5$): positive slope, but the graph falls.
  • B ($\frac{7}{3}x - 5$): positive slope, also incorrect.
  • C ($-\frac{3}{7}x - 5$): matches both the y-intercept and the point $(7, -8)$.
  • D ($-\frac{7}{3}x - 5$): correct sign but slope too steep; misses $(7, -8)$ badly.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
